rag_rules: Place timing constraints as assumptions in component type scopes when bounds are concrete and visible; use assign for implementation-level output bindings
Use AGREE annex blocks within component type or implementation scopes based on the nature of the contract (assumptions/guarantees vs. assignments)
Generate valid AGREE expressions using only visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex block
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and scoped to the owning component type or implementation
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex
Avoid encoding AADL property names directly as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for temporal and logical constraints
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for helper declarations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` only for output features or auxiliary variables in implementation scope
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions without visible typed ports
Do not place guarantees in implementation scope; use assumptions or assignments instead
Do not generate expressions involving Boolean or opaque data types unless they are explicitly typed in AGREE scope
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
